subject: math
instructions: Knock out the warm-up first — those are your quick wins. Then take on the hero training problems. Show your steps, and don't sweat the tricky ones; partway is still progress. You've got this.

questions.prompt: Rate: A hero can rescue 4 people every 5 minutes. At that rate, how many people can they rescue in 20 minutes?
Percent: A trainee lands 30% of 50 practice punches on target. How many hit the target?
Decimals: One training weight is 2.5 kg. How much do 4 of them weigh together?
Word problem: A hero-in-training runs a 1.2 km lap 5 times during practice. How far do they run in all?
Two-step word problem: A class of 20 students splits into teams of 4. Each team rescues 3 practice dummies. How many dummies are rescued in all?
